description: External Swift benchmark — Vapor pinned at tag 4.121.4. Queries cover the Vapor public surface (types, protocols, extensions, common-name disambiguation). Three queries are starred ★ as 'pathological lift' candidates per docs/swift-v1-handoff.md — the graph layer should show measurable positive lift on those (rg alone has a hard time disambiguating common names like `URI`, `init`, `Service` in a multi-thousand-file repo). Queries are designed to be literal substrings of the canonical def line (rg invokes with -F). Manual-trigger only via scripts/eval-swift-external.sh; NOT part of npm test.

cases.notes: Top-level Vapor type. Application.swift defines `public final class Application: Sendable`.
Protocol def for request middleware. AsyncMiddleware.swift is acceptable because it defines a sub-protocol. Query rewritten to literal substring `protocol Middleware`.

Common-name type. Request.swift defines `public final class Request`. The def must rank above use-sites; in practice on Vapor's hub-heavy structure, rg's ranking puts use-sites first — this case quantifies that gap.
Common-name type. Response.swift defines `public final class Response`.
EventLoopFuture's def lives in SwiftNIO (out-of-corpus). Vapor extends it in multiple files. ResponseCodable.swift's `extension EventLoopFuture: ResponseEncodable` is the most prominent.

★ pathological-lift query per docs/swift-v1-handoff.md. `Service` is a common name that appears in many Vapor files; the canonical file is Services/Service.swift even though it defines `extension Application { public struct Service }` (nested type, not top-level). The graph layer should help rank this above use-sites.
★ pathological-lift query. Initialism + common name. URI.swift defines `public struct URI: ... Codable, Sendable`.

★ pathological-lift query. `public init` matches every public initializer in the repo. The expectation isn't a single canonical answer — it's that key type files (Application, Request, Response, Routes) make top-5. Tests pipeline robustness on extreme common-name floods.

Multi-file extension target. Vapor has many `extension Application` blocks; the +15% extension-target boost should fire on each. Application.swift (the type def) is the relevantFile; the Application+*.swift extension files are acceptable.

Conformance lookup. Many Vapor types conform to Codable; the def lives in the Swift stdlib (out-of-corpus). URI.swift is a prominent conformer. minUsefulness deliberately low — this query has many spread-out hits.
Encoder protocol for Vapor content negotiation. Query rewritten to literal substring.
WebSocket types in Vapor. The WebSocket type itself lives in WebSocketKit; Vapor's files extend and route it.
Error protocol for HTTP aborts. Query rewritten to literal substring.
Validation protocol. Query rewritten to literal substring.
